original_text:
### Modular Assembly Conventions **[GENERAL — Solstice production rules]**
- Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding and declared background color; **no margins outside the section** — assembly is direct stacking.
- Section metadata must declare: background color, whether the section establishes `dermatomyositis (DM)`, footnote symbols consumed (†, ‡ assigned at assembly to avoid collisions), and whether it contains a CTA.
- Footnote symbols: superscript, assigned in order of appearance at **assembly time** — sections use placeholder tokens (e.g., `{{fn1}}`) rather than hard-coded daggers when more than one referenced claim exists.
- Locked components (header, footer, ISI) are inserted by the core team; sections adjacent to them must not include decorative elements that visually depend on what's above/below (e.g., a wave that's meant to continue into the footer).
- Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections (assembler responsibility, enabled by declared backgrounds).
- All colors in code as HEX; all sizing in px; inline CSS only (email client compatibility); `bgcolor` attributes duplicated for Outlook.

relations.note: Adjacency prohibition depends on the declared background color metadata to identify dark sections.

quotes: Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding
Each section is a self-contained `<table>`-based block at 600px with its own top/bottom 32px padding and declared background color; no margins outside the section — assembly is direct stacking.

rules.intent: Ensure reliable modular stacking and layout uniformity in email environments.
rules.rule_text: Each section is assembled as a self-contained table block at 600px width (dimension.canvas.width = 600px) with its own top/bottom 32px vertical padding (padding.section.vertical = 32px) and declared background color. No margins may exist outside of the section blocks; assembly is direct stacking.

quotes: Section metadata must declare: background color, whether the section establishes `dermatomyositis (DM)`, footnote symbols consumed (†, ‡ assigned at assembly to avoid collisions), and whether it contains a CTA.

rules.intent: Provide essential metadata inputs for algorithmic assembly, dynamic templating, and accessibility checking.
rules.rule_text: Section metadata must explicitly declare the background color, whether the section establishes dermatomyositis (DM), which footnote symbols are consumed, and whether the section contains a CTA.

quotes: Footnote symbols: superscript, assigned in order of appearance at assembly time — sections use placeholder tokens (e.g., {{fn1}}) rather than hard-coded daggers when more than one referenced claim exists.

rules.intent: Prevent numbering/symbol collisions across modular sections at runtime.
rules.rule_text: Footnote symbols must be superscript and assigned sequentially in order of appearance at assembly time. Sections must use placeholder tokens (such as {{fn1}}) instead of hard-coded symbols when multiple claims are present.

quotes: Locked components (header, footer, ISI) are inserted by the core team; sections adjacent to them must not include decorative elements that visually depend on what's above/below (e.g., a wave that's meant to continue into the footer).

rules.intent: Prevent layout breakage, misalignment, or jarring visual seams when generic modules stack against locked, brand-mandated templates.
rules.rule_text: Sections adjacent to locked components (such as the header, footer, or ISI) must not include decorative elements that visually rely on continuity with the adjacent component (e.g., a wave effect intended to carry over into the footer).

quotes: Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections
Dark-background sections must not be placed adjacent to other dark sections (assembler responsibility, enabled by declared backgrounds).

rules.intent: Maintain appropriate visual rhythm, contrast, and layout pacing.
rules.rule_text: Two dark-background sections must not be placed directly adjacent to one another during email assembly.

quotes: All colors in code as HEX; all sizing in px; inline CSS only (email client compatibility); `bgcolor` attributes duplicated for Outlook.

rules.intent: Ensure bulletproof rendering consistency across legacy and modern email clients.
rules.rule_text: All color references in the code must be defined using HEX values, all sizes must be in pixels (px), styling must apply inline CSS only, and bgcolor attributes on tables must be duplicated specifically to support older clients like Outlook.
